page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not feature local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing is relatively weak, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% tiers respectively. Conversely, multi-core execution is more robust, achieving average tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core benchmarks, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the average range, though the overall Passmark memory score is classified as weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1ne.4xlarge offers a dense memory allocation relative to its processing cores, making it suitable for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand leading-edge single-thread CPU speeds. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core performance compared to newer processor generations. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, redis caching layers, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks such as software compilation or light language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this server provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a contrast between weak single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities, with average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, static web serving, and Redis SET operations. Qualitatively, the instance offers high memory density relative to compute resources, presenting a tradeoff of lower single-thread speed for substantial memory capacity. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and moderate multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, showing average multi-core and weak single-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high memory density for database and caching workloads.
